title: put/get symmetry without ?field= is the right API shape
description:
The old design rejected put without ?field= with an error, making put and get asymmetric: get without ?field= returned a JSON blob, put without ?field= refused to accept one. The new design makes them symmetric: both speak JSON when no field is specified.

This is the correct API design: symmetric read/write pairs are the default reasonable expectation. The original restriction forced users to always specify ?field=, even for secrets that are naturally key-value maps. A contributor might suggest 'reverting to error for safety' — resist this: the symmetric design is deliberately chosen and the user must take responsibility for providing a valid JSON object.
